footer.title: Computational Algebra Problem Solver
footer.tagline: © {{year}} · Turning matrices into obedience since v1.0

home.title: Computational Algebra Problem Solver
home.subtitle: Choose one of the problems below to explore linear algebra, number theory, coding theory, and eigenvalues. Clean interface, clear explanations, accurate computations.

problem1.title: Triangular matrices
problem1.description: Verify whether the inverse of a triangular matrix is also triangular. Verify whether the product of two triangular matrices is also triangular.

problem2.title: Decomposition into symmetric and skew-symmetric matrices
problem2.description: Show that any matrix can be written uniquely as the sum of a symmetric matrix and a skew-symmetric matrix.

problem5.title: Rank and inverse using Gauss method
problem5.description: Compute the rank of a matrix using Gaussian elimination and, if possible, compute its inverse using the Gauss–Jordan method.

problem6.title: Order and idempotent element in (ℤₙ, ·)
problem6.description: Given (ℤₙ, ·), compute the order of an element and check whether it is idempotent modulo n.

problem11.title: Codeword validation using parity-check matrix
problem11.description: Read a parity-check matrix and a set of words. Display the ones that are codewords (zero syndrome).

problem12.title: Characteristic polynomial and eigenvalues (2×2, 3×3)
problem12.description: Compute the characteristic polynomial and the eigenvalues of a 2×2 or 3×3 matrix, displaying both the polynomial and its roots.

problem14.title: Euclidean algorithm, linear combination, and continued fraction
problem14.description: Using Euclid’s algorithm, compute gcd(d) for two natural numbers (a,b), write a linear combination of (a,b,d), and display the continued fraction of a/b.
